Identity.Name: StingHVACAirDensities
Identity.Definition: Air-density presets used by the HVAC sizing engine. Each preset selects a project-level kg/m³ assumed for duct pressure-drop and fan calculations. Picked from the STING HVAC Center header dropdown; consumed by FittingLossCalculator / Darcy-Weisbach pipework and MepAutoSizeDuctCommand.

Governance.StandardsBasis: CIBSE Guide C §4.3; ASHRAE Fundamentals 2021 Ch.1

Value.Definition: Standard — 1.20 kg/m³ at 20 °C, 101.325 kPa. STING default for office / clinical / retail HVAC.
Warm — 1.15 kg/m³ at ~30 °C. Used for warm climates, plant-room supply at elevated ambient.
Hot — 1.05 kg/m³ at ~50 °C. Industrial exhaust, kitchen extract, mechanical-room return.
Cold — 1.27 kg/m³ at 0 °C. Used for cold-climate fresh-air intakes and DOAS sizing.
Tropical — 1.10 kg/m³ at ~35 °C, 80% RH. STING default for tropical / equatorial deployments.
High Altitude — 0.95 kg/m³ at ~1500 m elevation, 20 °C. Engineering review required for mountain sites.
